title: Topic, Headers, Alternate, and Exchange-to-Exchange Routing · Contract Lens: Exchange Chain Cycle
summary: Routing keys encode unstable JSON, topic wildcards overlap unexpectedly, headers matching trusts absent fields, and unroutable messages vanish because mandatory and alternate behavior are not designed together. Planned artifact: Add and verify the exchange chain cycle increment in multi-strategy routing and unroutable-message table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use exchange-to-exchange bindings with bounded graph analysis, route evidence, permission checks, and cycle-safe topology review.
Route unmatched messages to an alternate exchange with observable ownership and distinguish it from mandatory returns.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Exchange Chain Cycle · Theory Exchange Chain Cycle
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 1257 in a regional incident workflow during one broker-node restart. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify primary, alternate, returned, and invalid routing outcomes; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement decision owner; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
